_note: swarf material palette — Phil 2026-04-15 r5. Starting defaults for HSS endmills on MR-1 / ShopBot Basic class prosumer CNCs. Published HSS speed/feed midranges CUT IN HALF for safety per Phil's rule. UI wiring TODO; data lives here so Phil can edit by hand.
_units: feed mm/min · plunge mm/min · spindle rpm · stepdown mm (absolute Z per pass) · stepover 0–1 fraction of tool diameter
_tools: by_tool keyed to endmill diameter in mm. 3.175 = 1/8 inch, 6.35 = 1/4 inch. HSS 2-flute is the reference; carbide can push 2–3× these numbers.

materials.name: hard wood
materials.note: oak, maple, walnut, cherry — sharp HSS leaves a clean edge. dust mask.

materials.name: foam
materials.note: rigid PU, EPS, blue/pink insulation — cuts like air. main risk is tool deflection on long flutes.

materials.name: aluminum 6061
materials.note: chips should curl not powder. keep RPM as low as the spindle allows. a mist of WD-40 or alcohol helps.

materials.name: polycarbonate
materials.note: Lexan and similar — gummy, melts if you feed too slow. single-flute O-flute is ideal; 2-flute HSS works if chips eject.

materials.name: mild steel
materials.note: occasional only. HSS on prosumer CNC is slow and punishing. small stepdowns, lots of lubricant, listen for chatter and back off.
